name: Whole Wheat Sourdough
recipeInstructions:
I used the whole wheat starter and experimented with your whole wheat bread recipe in my DAK today. It turned out great. Basically, I just halved your recipe and added a few things. When using whole wheat or rye flour, you can add more flour because it doesn't rise as much. Whenever I use whole wheat, etc., I add vital wheat gluten and lecithin to aid elasticity and help it rise.
This is what I put in the DAK and the order I used:
1. Turn the light/dark setting to 12 o'clock.
2. Set the menu to Sweet Bread (the automatic one, not the manual one).
3. At the beeps, add 3/4 cup Pecans (if walnuts are unavailable, use larger pieces as they remain intact during kneading).
You can keep experimenting with this. Have you tried adding raisins, oatmeal, or any kind of seeds? The DAK provides an easy way to prepare the dough, but I wanted to see what it would do when using sourdough starters. Thanks for sharing your recipe and for offering some beer starter when we get together! Whole wheat sourdough starter is great.
